description_long: This stone is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized rock with a rounded top and a broad, stable base. Its surface displays a striking pattern of layered bands in shades of brown, beige, and off-white, suggesting sedimentary or metamorphic origins. The stone's texture appears rough and unpolished, with visible natural striations and subtle undulations across its surface. Some areas are smoother, likely due to weathering, while others retain a more granular, gritty appearance. The coloration and banding are uneven, with darker, more pronounced lines running horizontally around the midsection. This stone would be a prominent, naturalistic feature in an urban park, garden, or as a decorative barrier along a pathway.
description: A medium-sized, irregular stone with layered brown and beige bands and a rough, natural surface. Its rounded top and broad base make it visually striking and stable.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broadest face, showcasing prominent horizontal banding and a gently rounded top.
description_view_1: The stone appears more conical from this angle, with a smoother, lighter upper section and less visible banding.
description_view_2: This side reveals a flatter face with sharply defined, parallel layers and a squarer profile.
description_view_3: The stone's rear is more tapered and less detailed, with subtle color transitions and a smoother surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Distinct horizontal banding and layering with irregular, natural striations
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with some smoother, weathered areas indicating moderate exposure to the elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, providing a subtle contrast to manicured lawns or paved surfaces
